title: Recover the Min Heap After Deletion of Duplicate Elements

statement.markdown:
You are given an integer array nums and an integer k. The size of the array is at most 4 * 10^4. You are allowed to remove at most k elements from the array to recover a min heap. Return the max value that can be generated by the min heap after removals.

Constraints:
  1 <= nums.length <= 4 * 10^4
  0 <= nums[i] < 2^31 - 1
  0 <= k < nums.length
  It's guaranteed that the final answer will not exceed 2^31 - 1 and will not have two same values.
statement.inputFormat:
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers, n, and k, representing the size of the array and the number of elements to remove.
The second line contains n space-separated integers.

Example:
5 2
10 20 30 40 50
statement.outputFormat: Output the max value that can be generated by the min heap.
statement.notes: The size of the array is at most 4 * 10^4. You are allowed to remove at most k elements from the array to recover a min heap.

examples.explanation: Remove the 30 and 40 to recover a min heap with 20 as the max value.

hints.text: First hint text: Use a min heap data structure to recover the min heap.

functionSignature.python: def recoverMinHeap(self, nums: list[int], k: int) -> None:
